tools.name: MitoMAT Full Body Red Light Therapy Mat

faq.question: What are the discrete visual symbols reported in the registry?
faq.answer: Discrete visual symbols are bounded, structured patterns resembling alphabetic characters, geometric shapes, and glyphs reported during 650 nm coherent light exposure combined with N,N-DMT administration. Davis et al. (2021) documented 87% inter-subject consistency in symbol observations.

faq.question: Is the 650 nm laser protocol safe?
faq.answer: Coherent light exposure at 650 nm wavelength and ≤5 mW power output falls within Class 2 laser safety standards. Ocular safety requires avoiding direct beam exposure. N,N-DMT administration carries standard psychedelic contraindications.

faq.question: How does 650 nm light interact with visual perception?
faq.answer: Red wavelength photons (650 nm) penetrate retinal tissue and may interact with visual processing during altered consciousness states. Timmermann et al. (2019) documented altered visual cortex activity patterns.

faq.question: Is N,N-DMT legal?
faq.answer: N,N-dimethyltryptamine is classified as Schedule I controlled substance in United States and most jurisdictions. Legal exceptions exist for religious use in specific contexts. This project does not provide legal advice.

faq.question: What equipment is required for the protocol?
faq.answer: Reported protocol uses: (1) 650 nm laser pointer (≤5 mW), (2) 500-1000 lines/mm diffraction grating, (3) N,N-DMT vaporization device. Goler (2025) pilot documented symbol observations across 3,000+ replicators.

faq.question: Can I contribute to the registry anonymously?
faq.answer: Yes. The registry accepts both anonymous and authenticated submissions. Anonymous contributors draw symbols on 100×100 pixel canvas with structured metadata. All submissions released under CC-BY-4.0 license.

faq.question: How quickly should I document symbols after an experience?
faq.answer: Strassman (2001) documented rapid memory decay: 60% detail loss within 15 minutes, 90% within 2 hours. Immediate documentation (within 5 minutes) maximizes accuracy.

faq.question: What is the inter-subject consistency rate?
faq.answer: Davis et al. (2021) reported 87% consistency in visual symbol observations among participants using identical laser specifications. This consistency suggests systematic documentation value.

faq.question: Are the symbols related to form constants?
faq.answer: Classical form constants (tunnels, spirals) arise from V1 visual cortex architecture. Reported laser protocol symbols differ: alphabetic-like characters and discrete glyphs not predicted by V1 models.

faq.question: How can I access the complete dataset?
faq.answer: Full registry dataset available at /data.json (machine-readable JSON endpoint), updated in real-time. Released under CC-BY-4.0 license for academic research and independent verification.

faq.question: What contraindications exist for N,N-DMT use?
faq.answer: Standard psychedelic contraindications apply: personal or family history of psychosis, schizophrenia, bipolar disorder, severe anxiety disorders. Consult licensed medical professionals before use.

faq.question: Who maintains the DMT Code registry?
faq.answer: Community-maintained volunteer project. Team includes MSc Neuroscience holders with published work in visual perception and psychopharmacology. No formal institutional affiliation.
